transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hey, how's it going?
Good, how are you doing?
Not too bad, sorry, it took me a minute.
I've been at WeWork today, so I'd like to go find a small room here.
All good.
Nice to get out and be around other people, I imagine.
Yeah, we've got, one of our teams is having an offsite here in Chicago, so I thought I'd pop in.
Mm-hmm.
Nice.
So, yeah, it's been good.
Hello.
Hey, Megan.
How's it going?
I'm good.
How are you doing?
Good.
Thanks.
Rolling along.
Busy week here, as I imagine, for a lot of us prepping for holiday stuff.
But I appreciate you guys jumping into a call to go over a few things.
Yeah, no problem.
Yeah.
I've gotten agenda prepped.
But if there's anything pressing top of mind that you guys want to discuss, feel free to just bring it up at any point.
I know I've done kind of a quick recap, sending lots of messages in Slack.
This was while trying to make sure that we're all kind of on the same page with where things are at.
This week, as we've talked about, like really prioritizing getting those JSON files finished once we get any final feedback from Chris.
I know he's working on that today as well.
So I'm working with my team, so long as we get like.
Any final adjustments that we need to do, we should be able to get those over to you by Wednesday at the end of the day.
And then we also have those 10 new full Google Docs in progress that we'll send.
So it's like new content that we're sending over to you guys for review.
And part of those 10 are the Includes Blubable, which is the one that I sent earlier this morning.
Any questions about that layout for this week?
No, I think that's good.

Again, I think one just, I don't know if it's feedback for you or feedback for our team, is like I just want to like continually like reiterate that I want to make sure that you're not waiting on me or Chris on anything.
Like I just, I want to make sure that we continue to make momentum.
I know Chris has had a couple like urgent things pop up, but he's going to review some stuff.
Sort of like it sits with him.
Yeah.
The only thing I would ask is just like, let's continue to over communicate so we can continue to move forward.
Totally.
And my thinking for the JSON files, like, even if we don't get any final feedback at the end of the day, we'll go ahead and produce the others.
And then we can tweak those if we need to.
But I'd rather get you guys content rather than have it, you know, sitting in a backlog waiting.
Yeah.
In, in vile means, like, if you haven't heard from me or Chris in like 24 hours, especially like leading up to the holidays, just shoot us a ping.
Like it could very well be like something popped up, Chris got pulled away, whatever.
We're not going to be offended if you, or I certainly won't be offended.
I can't speak for Chris, but like, you know, just ping us if it's been a day and you haven't heard from us.
Yeah.
Yeah, we'll do.
And, and same goes here.
Like if there's something that you, that we had agreed on and you're waiting on, um, or like was unclear, please let me know.
Um, definitely, definitely trying to over communicate in this busy time for all of us.
Cool.
So, so I guess like while.
We're down this.
Sorry.
I know you have an agenda.
just like, let me, you correct me if I'm wrong here.
So like, as it stands today, we had one JSON created for OpenAI's API.
There were some adjustments that Chris that asked for.
You reproduced JSONs for six of the pages or five of the pages, and it's with him to review the formatting for those.
So right now, I didn't do OpenAI because we're still waiting on that feedback.
So I did the perplexity page because that one was finalized.
And what we had talked about last week was aligning on that one and then producing the others.
So that's the only one that we have shared so far.
And then by tomorrow, we're going to share the six others.
You know, seeing if Chris sends any feedback at the end of today, which I had pinged him about this morning, we will implement that.
Otherwise, we're just going to go ahead with the adjustments that he and I have already talked about late last night for that.
And so then we'll send all those JSON files over tomorrow, likely midday.
And then if there's any edits on those JSON files, then we can take those and tweak them.
Okay.
Okay.

And one thing I want ask Katya is if you can just make sure you're adding to like the operations checklist before it comes to us for review, that we're just doing a final check on like this aligns to the format that was discussed with Chris.
That'd be great.
Yeah.
We will.
So what I've been doing in the way our pipeline is working right now is it's got that template format that should have been discussed, that it's following and should be plugging that in.
And we are doing a final check to like try and make sure it's following all those pieces.
I know with some of the recent edits, like with nested lists, the pipeline threw in some new sub points that were causing a little bit of an issue.
It was something that we talked about last night.
Yeah.
And so resolving those final few details to make sure that everything is more standardized.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Thanks.
Yeah, no worries.
Sorry.
It's all good.
So just to reiterate again for clarification.
So all nine of the outstanding JSON files will be delivered to us midday tomorrow-ish.
Yes.
All right.
Midday tomorrow.
Okay, cool.
And then, yeah, I do have on my list to take a look at the lovable page.
So you should have that by the end of the day.
Like, totally understand if we can't get that tomorrow.
I just want to make sure that's a fast follow.
And, yeah, I guess just on the comments that our growth team added to the docs, I assume those were pretty straightforward, but any clarification needed on those?
I have not had a chance to dig into those yet today.
I am going to look at that later this afternoon.
And then if I do have any clarification, follow up ASAP.
Okay.
Yeah, I took a look at them.
you.
don't know.
I felt like they were pretty straightforward.
Yeah, pretty clear, but I'll do a double check.
if I need to, like, I'll at you if there's anything that feels a little bit out of whack.
Okay, cool.
I have something right after this, but then I'll jump into that.
That's one of my priorities for later today to look that over and make sure that gets actioned.
Yeah, sounds good.
Yeah, and then for Lovable, my thinking there is, worst case, we should be able to get that over to you Friday, depending on the number of edits that we end up needing to do based on your feedback.
But ideally, you should be able to get that over to you, like, Thursday.
But by the end of the week, you should have that JSON file.

So, one thing that I did, I figured it would be good for us to talk about, like, as we're getting really close to these final alignments on some of the edits that you've sent over and, like, any final tweaks with Craig.

Is looking ahead at sort of establishing kind of a really clear cadence between all of us in terms of like when we send content, when you get feedback back to us, and then when we convert the JSON file.
Because I figure if we get more of a schedule, that will also help with our like communication and planning.
So the proposal that I had sort of put together, and we can tweak these days depending on what you guys feel like you need on your end.
But if we send new content every Wednesday at the end of the day, and then by the next Monday, you will get us any revisions, then we can have those JSON files converted again by that next Wednesday.
So it'd be about a week out depending on revisions.
And as fewer and fewer revisions are needed on the Google Docs, we can tighten that timeline.
But wanted to make sure that you guys felt like you had enough time to review those five when we send them.
And then a couple days for us to convert the JSON files once we get them back.
Okay.
Yeah.
I think that's fine.
Yeah, I think that's a fine timeline.
think the only like immediate reaction I get is like, I don't, I would just assume get a call at the end of week so that your team can start Monday morning.
So like in some cases that might be Friday afternoon for me, in some cases it might just be over the weekend, which is fine.
But I would just want to make sure that we don't like waste a business day kind of waiting on us.

If you, yeah, like if you get the content over to us, like Wednesday morning, let's three business days, I would think that that's probably sufficient enough time to review, especially if it's not like a hard, like, are we going to do batches of 10?
Like what's the volume?
So it would be batches of five.
We've got 10 coming to you this week because of the holiday, which I, yeah, I had flagged.
Last week, basically, we're doing content for this week and content for next week.
And then we've got the quiet week, the 29th.
Okay.
Yeah.
So five should be fine.
Obviously, like, going to be a little bit different over the holidays, but like 10 is great.
Think five per week seems feasible enough.
I'll call it like you can call it like end of week, but realistically, like it might be like Sunday afternoon or whatever.
So as long as like, I setting the expectation with your team that they can start Monday morning, start work Monday morning is probably feasible.
Yeah, I will.
I'll connect with my team on my end and make sure that with like their other content calendars they've got going.
But I think that that should probably work.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Cool.
And then as far as looking at like revisions that come back on those 10 that we send, because we do have quite a few team members out between Christmas and New Year's.
So for those 10, a...
If we get revisions back, we can probably apply them over that quiet week on the 29th.
But realistically, we'll probably be sending the JSON files that first week of January.
We want to make sure that we're all on the same page there.
Cool.
One other thing that I'm working on to help bring a little bit of clarity for all of us just on where content stands is updating our Airtable, like our content OS.
I want to basically create a content calendar that shows everything a month out so that you guys aren't wondering which content we're prioritizing.
So this is one of my projects over the holidays, quiet week to fine-tune this.
But today I wanted to flag, we have this backlog of content.
This is from a list.
You guys sent us a list and we had a list and we compiled those.
As I'm building out this content calendar, we don't have to do this on the call.
could be easier to do it async.
But I would...
I'd love to know if you guys have priority companies that you would like us to focus on, or even just like groups of companies that we can use to strategize how we're approaching that.
So we make sure that we're investing our time where you guys really want it.
Yeah, that sounds good.
When do you want us to complete that?
Like, are you going to share this with us in early January, and then we do that exercise?
Or what are you thinking?
Kind of depends on your capacity.
What I was thinking is that I would start to put together a calendar, like, in that quiet week and share it with you.
And then if we need to adjust, we can totally do that in January, just so have a proposal.
But if you guys have time to get just some basic notes on priorities over to me by, like, the week of New Year's, then I'll be using that week to help build this out.
Okay.
I just wasn't sure how much you guys had on your plates with holidays.
What do you think, Boyle?
Do you think you've...
Be able to do a pass at, like, maybe not prioritizing the entire list, but, like, you know, selecting the more immediate just cursory.
Even if I just had, like, 10 to start with, I could build up, you know, a couple months-ish from there.
Why don't we, how many are in this list?
There's 62 currently in the backlog.
Okay, so let's, yeah, that should be fine.
When is our next meeting?
It'd be that first full week of January.
So the 5th is a Tuesday, I think, right?
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah, I think that should be fine.
If we don't, I'll at least do 10.
Like, that should be easy enough.
If not, I'll maybe just try and do, like, the top 50 so that we can just keep rolling.
But let me, yeah, that should be fine.
Okay.
leave it at that.
Can you, what, what's, oh, Airtable, okay.
Okay.
So I feasibly have access to this, right?
You do.
And I can send you the link.
I'll send a call recap after this that highlights any action items that we've talked about.
So we're all on the same page.
And what I'll do for you is I'll add a little column here where you can just flag it as a priority piece.
And then you can just do like high, medium, low priority.
And then I can use those two to do assignments.
Sounds good.
Cool.
Otherwise, the only other piece that I had that I wanted to talk about is considering shifting to biweekly calls.
I know it's kind of the cadence we've been on because we ended up, you guys had to cancel one a few weeks ago.
But come January meeting every other week.
so we'll use like Slack and Async for, you know, weekly product, project updates.
I'll be sending those Monday sort of overviews of what we did and what we're going to do the following week.
So there's lots of transparency there.
And then our call time can be spent.

Really digging into like strategic decisions and, you looking at analytics as we start to publish and doing some of the reporting so that we can dig into the nitty gritty there rather than our calls being all about, here's what we did.
Cool.
Yeah, that sounds good.
Awesome.
And, you know, if there's ever a need to add another call for a week because we don't want to skip one, we can always add it back into the calendar.
Okay, that sounds good.
Cool.
Anything else on top of mind for you all?
Cool.
I think that's good.
Awesome.
Quick, fairly quick meeting.
Thanks for the time and chatting.
I will send a recap of all of this a bit later today and I'll look over that commentary, the feedback on those four articles that got sent over and we'll start cracking along.
Yeah.
And one, I guess just, so Jack on our growth team, he dropped comments on the wrong.
So I'm just going to transfer those over now.
So just FYI, I might like accept a couple.
So I would just suggest for the ChatGPT and OpenAI API docs, if your team can just do like another final read through on those, just to make sure everything got transferred over.
Yep.
I will make sure everything is on there now.
Okay, cool.
It's only like two comments, so I think it should be fine.
Okay, cool.
And as we calibrate, we'll need, you know, less and less versions, so that'll help too.
Yep.
Awesome.
Cool.
Great.
Yeah, if there's anything else, feel free to ping me.
I am around.
And then, I think I mentioned this already, but I am out most of the week of Christmas.
I'll be lightly online.
And then I will be more available through the week of New Year's for like Async updates and things.
Cool.
Thanks, Katya.
Enjoy your holiday.
Yeah, thanks.
You too.
Thanks.
Thanks, Katya.
Thanks, Katya.
Thanks,

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align on content delivery, establish a new cadence, and prioritize the backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**JSON Delivery:** All 9 outstanding JSON files are due midday tomorrow (Dec 17), unblocked by Chris's final feedback. The "Includes Blubable" JSON is due by Friday.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=493.0)
  - [**New Cadence:** A weekly cycle was approved: 5 new Google Docs sent Wed EOD, feedback due Fri EOD, and JSONs delivered the following Wed.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=590.0)
  - [**Backlog Prioritization:** Will will prioritize the 62-item content backlog in Airtable by the first week of January to guide future content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
  - [**Meeting Cadence:** Weekly syncs will become bi-weekly in January to shift focus from status updates to strategic discussions.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=987.0)

## Topics

### Content Delivery & JSON Conversion

  - [**Immediate Deliverables:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=225.0)
      - [**9 JSON Files:** Due midday Dec 17.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=493.0)
          - [**Rationale:** To maintain momentum, delivery is unblocked by Chris's final feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=273.0)
          - [**Process:** GrowthX will use existing adjustments discussed with Chris; any future edits will be handled post-delivery.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=306.0)
      - [**10 New Google Docs:** Due Dec 17 EOD.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=248.0)
          - [**Rationale:** This batch covers content for both this week and next, ahead of the holiday period.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=711.0)
      - [**"Includes Blubable" JSON:** Due by Friday, Dec 19.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=505.0)
  - [**Quality Control:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=432.0)
      - [Megan requested a final format check be added to the GrowthX operations checklist before JSONs are delivered.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=432.0)
      - [**Rationale:** To ensure all files consistently adhere to the agreed-upon structure, preventing issues like the recent nested list formatting error.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=432.0)

### New Weekly Cadence

  - [**Approved Workflow:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=590.0)
      - [**Wed EOD:** GrowthX sends 5 new Google Docs.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0)
      - [**Fri EOD:** Metronome provides feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0)
          - [**Rationale:** This accelerated timeline allows GrowthX to start JSON conversion on Monday, avoiding a wasted business day.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=669.0)
      - [**Next Wed:** GrowthX delivers the 5 corresponding JSON files.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0)
  - [**Holiday Exception:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=766.0)
      - [Revisions for the 10 docs sent this week will be applied during the "quiet week" (Dec 29), with JSONs delivered the first week of January.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=766.0)

### Content Backlog Prioritization

  - [**Problem:** The 62-item content backlog requires prioritization to ensure GrowthX's efforts align with Metronome's strategic goals.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
  - [**Solution:** Katya will update the Airtable "Content OS" to create a 1-month content calendar.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
  - [**Action:** Will will review the backlog in Airtable and assign "High," "Medium," or "Low" priorities to guide the calendar.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=898.0)
      - [**Deadline:** First week of January.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=898.0)
      - [**Scope:** Will will prioritize at least the top 10, but may prioritize the full list of 62.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=898.0)

### Meeting Cadence Shift

  - [**Decision:** Weekly syncs will become bi-weekly starting in January.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=987.0)
  - [**Rationale:** To shift the focus from status updates to higher-value strategic discussions, analytics reviews, and reporting.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=987.0)
  - [**Communication:** Weekly progress updates will continue asynchronously via Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=987.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX (Katya):**](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=225.0)
      - [Deliver 9 outstanding JSONs by Dec 17 midday.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=493.0)
      - [Deliver 10 new Google Docs by Dec 17 EOD.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=248.0)
      - [Deliver "Includes Blubable" JSON by Dec 19.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=505.0)
      - [Add a final format check to the operations checklist for all JSONs.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=432.0)
      - [Update Airtable with a priority column for the content backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=957.0)
      - [Send a meeting recap with the Airtable link.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=957.0)
  - [**Metronome (Will):**](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=898.0)
      - [Prioritize the 62-item content backlog in Airtable by the first week of January.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=898.0)
      - [Transfer Jack's comments to the correct ChatGPT and OpenAI API docs.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=1073.0)
      - [Review the "Includes Blubable" Google Doc and provide feedback by EOD today.](https://fathom.video/share/oXvFs6-G2iG66qJVN2SZ4LmNM6LC4dnk?tab=summary&timestamp=505.0)

action_items.description: Add JSON format-check to ops checklist; ensure final check before sending to Metronome
Review growth-team comments on 4 docs; follow up w/ Will if unclear
Review Lovable doc; send feedback to Katya by EOD
Align w/ team on Wed content-send + EOW revisions cadence
Build 1-month content calendar in Airtable during quiet week; share w/ Will & Megan
Add priority column to Airtable backlog; ask Will to flag ~10–50
Send Monday project updates in Slack to Will & Megan
Transfer Jack’s comments to correct docs; ask Katya’s team to re-read ChatGPT & OpenAI API
